skill.name: bitbucket-automation
skill.description: Automate Bitbucket repositories, pull requests, branches, issues, and workspace management through Rube MCP and Composio.
skill.summary: Guides Bitbucket automation workflows through Rube MCP tools for repositories, pull requests, branches, and issues.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, certificate, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, tool names, BBQL examples, and SHA1 commit-hash documentation. The confirmed risks are legitimate third-party MCP network access and documented irreversible Bitbucket delete operations, so the skill should publish with a warning.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP and OAuth Access
medium_findings.description: The skill requires Rube MCP and instructs users to connect Bitbucket through RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS. This is intended functionality, but it grants a third-party MCP service access to Bitbucket workspace, repository, pull request, issue, and member data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The MCP endpoint and Bitbucket connection workflow are explicit in the skill instructions. The behavior appears legitimate for Bitbucket automation, but it materially expands data access through an external service.

medium_findings.title: Irreversible Bitbucket Delete Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill documents repository and issue deletion workflows and states that these actions are permanent. This is not malicious, but users could lose repository or issue data if the assistant runs these operations without clear confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The delete capabilities and warnings are directly documented. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the skill also tells the assistant to confirm before delete operations.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick findings come from Markdown inline code spans that name Rube and Bitbucket MCP tools, parameters, enum values, and examples. No evidence found of Ruby code, shell commands, command substitution, or local process execution in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these lines are documentation bullets and tables, not executable code. The skill file contains only Markdown guidance and MCP tool names.

low_findings.title: Crypto, Certificate, and Reconnaissance Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto alerts correspond to commit hash or parameter documentation, and the sensitive or reconnaissance alerts correspond to BBQL examples such as project.key and repository field names. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, private key handling, certificate files, or host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible in parameter and pitfall documentation. It does not create or use keys, algorithms, certificates, or system discovery commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Network Dependency
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs users to add https://rube.app/mcp and authenticate Bitbucket through Rube MCP. This network dependency is required for the skill but should be disclosed before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external MCP endpoint is explicitly documented. The risk is clear but expected for a Bitbucket automation integration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Destructive Actions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes permanent delete workflows for repositories and issues. These actions require strict user confirmation and careful target resolution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The destructive actions and confirmation warning are directly listed. The pattern is risky because it can cause permanent loss if misused.

content.value_statement: Bitbucket work often requires many API steps and exact identifiers. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for repositories, pull requests, branches, issues, and workspace data.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and Bitbucket OAuth connection checks.
Lists workspaces, repositories, branches, pull requests, issues, and workspace members.
Creates repositories, branches, pull requests, issues, issue comments, and pull request comments.
Fetches pull request details, unified diffs, and diff statistics for review workflows.
Documents Bitbucket parameter formats for UUIDs, BBQL filters, pagination, and issue states.
Warns about irreversible delete operations and large diff handling.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Bitbucket connection before workflows can run.
Does not provide local Bitbucket API code or standalone scripts.
Depends on current Rube tool schemas, so tools must be searched before use.
Delete operations remain permanent and require careful human confirmation.

use_cases.title: Prepare Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Create a pull request after resolving the workspace, repository, branches, title, reviewers, and destination branch.

use_cases.title: Review Repository Changes
use_cases.description: Fetch pull request details, diffs, and diff statistics before writing top-level or inline review comments.

use_cases.title: Manage Workspace Hygiene
use_cases.description: List repositories, inspect branches, track issues, and manage repository metadata with explicit safeguards for destructive actions.

prompt_templates.title: List My Bitbucket Repositories
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Bitbucket automation skill to find my available workspaces, then list repositories in the workspace I choose. Search Rube tools first.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Bitbucket pull request from [source branch] to [destination branch] in [workspace]/[repository]. Resolve required IDs first and show me the planned request before submitting.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect pull request [ID] in [workspace]/[repository]. Fetch the diff and diffstat with a safe max_chars limit, then summarize risks and suggest review comments.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Issues and Branches
prompt_templates.prompt: For [workspace]/[repository], list open issues, stale branches, and recent pull requests. Use pagination and BBQL filters where useful, then recommend cleanup actions without deleting anything.

output_examples.input: Create a pull request for feature/login in the mobile-api repository.
output_examples.output: The assistant resolves the workspace and repository, verifies feature/login and the target branch, prepares the pull request title and reviewers, and asks for approval before creating it.

output_examples.input: Review pull request 42 and comment on risky changes.
output_examples.output: The assistant fetches the pull request details, diff, and diffstat, summarizes changed files, identifies review concerns, and drafts concise Bitbucket comments.

output_examples.input: List critical open issues assigned to me.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks the repository issue tracker, applies state and priority filters, handles pagination, and returns a short triage list.

best_practices: Search Rube tools before each workflow so parameter schemas match the current MCP implementation.
Resolve workspace, repository, branch, member, issue, and pull request identifiers before making changes.
Ask for explicit user confirmation before repository deletion, issue deletion, or public visibility changes.
anti_patterns: Do not assume a repository default branch is named main.
Do not pass reviewer usernames where Bitbucket requires UUID objects.
Do not fetch very large pull request diffs without setting a max_chars limit.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Bitbucket API token?
faq.answer: The skill expects Rube MCP and a Bitbucket connection through RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS. Authentication is handled through that MCP workflow.

faq.question: Can it create pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents how to resolve workspaces, repositories, branches, reviewers, and required pull request parameters.

faq.question: Can it review pull request diffs?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides use of pull request detail, diff, and diffstat tools, including a max_chars limit for large diffs.

faq.question: Can it delete repositories or issues?
faq.answer: Yes, but those actions are permanent. The skill instructs assistants to confirm with the user before executing delete operations.

faq.question: Why should tools be searched first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP schemas can change. Searching tools first reduces parameter mistakes and stale assumptions.

faq.question: Is this a local command-line automation script?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown skill that guides an assistant to use Bitbucket tools exposed through Rube MCP.
